title: [v4.2] (Windows) Drop stale config value resulting in asymmetric config
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #15638

/assign n1hility

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

```release-note
Fixed log follow operations on podman machine on Windows
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *openshift-cherrypick-robot* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign edsantiago for approval by writing `/assign @edsantiago` in a comment. For more information see:[The Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.2/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago Looks like it's barfing on the tests-required bit. I forget - can we edit the PR description and add that in / force a rebase, or do we have to change the actual commit message?

I can't remember either. I've edited the github message and pressed re-run, we'll find out. (If it fails, you'll need to edit the actual commit message and re-push, but not necessarily rebase unless you want to)
Alright, I'm just going to backport manually as opposed to trying to work with the bot's branch.
